message:
So last week my kid asked, 'Mom, where's the flashlight?' during a random power blip. I confidently said, 'In the junk drawer!' Spoiler: it wasn't. Cue 10 minutes of me stumbling around in the dark, laughing at myself, while my family just... waited. 😅

That's when it hit me—being prepared doesn't have to be this serious, military-grade thing. It's just... knowing where your stuff is. Having a basic plan. Maybe keeping some water that you actually remember exists.

The funny part? Once I actually rounded up a few essentials and put them somewhere I could find them, life got easier. Not scarier. Just... simpler.

What's your go-to 'oops, I wasn't ready' moment? I feel like we've all got one!
